characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Badass Teacher: A downplayed example but Tom isn’t afraid to throw himself into danger when his kids need him. He’s a guy who’d rather resolve things with empathy and patience, but will absolutely step in physically when a student is about to get hurt, whether he’s breaking up violent fights, confrontingEarl Kelly, or literally wrestling Kyle Stack off a rooftop. Good Stepmother: He gets along well with Chlo and Mika and becomes their foster fatherafter Izzie is killed. Long-Lost Relative: Has a son he never knew about in Josh. Oh, Crap!:Tom's reaction in series 1, episode 7 says it all when Lorna catches him and Izzie Redpath kissing right in front of her. Politically Motivated Teacher: Downplayed. Tom's left-wing politics are well-known, but the only time it really comes to the fore is when he attempts to quit in Series 8 in opposition to the school being made fee-paying.

characters.name: Series One
characters.description: Badass Teacher: A downplayed example but Tom isn’t afraid to throw himself into danger when his kids need him. He’s a guy who’d rather resolve things with empathy and patience, but will absolutely step in physically when a student is about to get hurt, whether he’s breaking up violent fights, confrontingEarl Kelly, or literally wrestling Kyle Stack off a rooftop. Good Stepmother: He gets along well with Chlo and Mika and becomes their foster fatherafter Izzie is killed. Long-Lost Relative: Has a son he never knew about in Josh. Oh, Crap!:Tom's reaction in series 1, episode 7 says it all when Lorna catches him and Izzie Redpath kissing right in front of her. Politically Motivated Teacher: Downplayed. Tom's left-wing politics are well-known, but the only time it really comes to the fore is when he attempts to quit in Series 8 in opposition to the school being made fee-paying.

characters.name: Series Two
characters.description: The Ace: Takes alotof subjects, and is by far Waterloo Road's strongest student. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Her first boyfriend is her drug supplier Lewis, and she falls hard for Earl because his lack of respect for rules and adults is exciting. She dumps Earl when she sees his level of "bad" extends from rebellious toLack of Empathyand he shoots her for it. Heel–Face Turn: Maxine is a drug-addicted child left behind in Series 2, but she becomes a rounded and popular character when Steph takes her under her wing. Attention Whore: Tells Donte she is pregnant just as he tries to save Chlo from a fire, but he doesn't believe her. The truth is never revealed, at least in the original run. The Bus Came Back: Returns in 2025.

characters.name: Series Three
characters.description: Hooker with a Heart of Gold: Former hooker, anyway. She's a strong believer in the importance of education and its ability to turn any person's life around. I Was Young and Needed the Money: Rachel's justification for her chequered past. Surprisingly, the school community accepts it, and rallies behind her. Non-Giving-Up School Guy: One of the more empathetic headteachers the school has had. Samaritan Syndrome: Refuses to leave the burning school until she's certain everyone is out, nearly dying as a result. And in the very next episode, when the school is evacuated by a gun scare, she runs back inside, unarmed, to talk the young gunman down rather than risk him being hurt by the Armed Response Unit.Eddie:You can't save everyone.Rachel:I can try. Scars Are Ugly: She's hesitant to be more than colleagues with Eddie because of the burns sustained in season three's fire.

characters.name: Series Four
characters.description: Hot Teacher: Danielle and Aleesha can't stop drooling over him, even stealing his workout shorts. I Coulda Been a Contender!: He was on the fast track to be a pro boxer, only to get injured and become a bitter has-been. Vicariously Ambitious: It becomes clear that Rob sees Bolton's boxing career as his second chance for one as time goes on. He trains him relentlessly, pushes him with physical and verbal abuse, and gives him pills "to focus" that are almost certainly steroids. Only Known by Their Nickname: The staff and students only really call him 'Security Dave'. Big Bad: He was the closest to the main threat in season 4 as he beat up Marley andnearly destroyed Waterloo Road with a stolen excavator in a drunken rage.

characters.name: Series Five
characters.description: Hot for Teacher: A frequent target of this, which causes him no end of trouble. Mistaken for Pedophile: Rachel warns him to keep his distance in his very first episode precisely to avoid this, but he just can't seem to help blurring the boundaries. His innocent, friendly gestures towards some of the girls are horribly misinterpreted by Lindsay,who generally mistrusts men due to her sexually abusive father,and things come to a head when, with only the best of intentions, he allows neglected pupil Jodi to stay the night at his place. No Good Deed Goes Unpunished: Lets a neglected schoolgirl stay the night at his place when her mother absconds. How do you suppose that went when his boss Karen found out? Parental Substitute: To Jodi Allen. Control Freak: Max doesn't keep anyone who won't obey him without question.

characters.name: Series Six
characters.description: Parents as People: A loving but deeply flawed mother. She struggles with listening to her husband and kids, and pressured Bex to be perfect and fixates on her after she runs away from home. Pure Is Not Good: The problem with his homeschooling methods. Isolating his children made them book smart but enable to thrive in the outside world. Ruth is arrogant and disrespectful, while Jonah falls in love with his teacher. Straw Feminist: At her worst. She advocates for gender equality butassumes the worst of male studentswhile completely missing the signs that her friend Cesca is carrying on with a student. Post-Stress Overeating: He gorges himself on junk food to cope with his family's arguing, then throws up to cope with the shame. I Just Want to Be Loved: Her parents are too caught up in their own problems to notice her, so sheReally Gets Aroundto fill the void. The main target of this is Chris, and she went as far as showing up at his home and trying to make him jealous before accepting Chris wouldn't (knowingly) cross that line.

characters.name: Series Seven
characters.description: Mercy Kill: Gives a fatal overdose to his father, with whom he had reconnected via Sian. He was seriously ill, and had wanted to go with anI Cannot Self-Terminate. Despite being arrested and with a witness in his father's carer, the policedrop the chargesthe next episode. Non-Giving-Up School Guy: Deconstructed. He hated school due to coming from an abusive household, but an inspiring English teacher put him on the straight and narrow. He has since become a successful headteacher, and wants to do the same for other kids. Dark and Troubled Past: He grew up in foster care and suffered lasting abuse in at least one of them. Geek Physique: A portly, unathletic math teacher. Good Parents: He fosters and later adopts Kevin. It's a rough start because of the boy's trust issues, but Chalky wins him over to the point Kevin legally changes his last name to Chalk.

characters.name: Series Eight
characters.description: Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Pushy, close-minded and prone to snap judgements, but she cares for her students and accepts fault when proven wrong. Kindhearted Cat Lover: Has a cat named Mitzi, and a subplot of Series 8 happens when it gets stolen. Alcoholic Parent: She depends on alcohol bottles hidden throughout her house and the school to function. Child by Rape: Connor is the product of her father-in-law forcing himself on her. She never intended to tell him, but Connor overhears her admit it to Michael. Improperly Paranoid: She is convinced that everyone has it out for her and it's her and Connor against the world, no doubt because of her drinking.

characters.name: Series Nine
characters.description: Hypocrite: Gives Nikki plenty of grief for cheating on her sister with Hector one timeonly to do the same thing multiple times. Manchild: Sue treats her job like a hobby, pouts to get her way, and fights with disruptive pupils instead of disciplining them. WhenSimon leaves her, his departing words are basically that she's overdue to grow up. Spoiled Brat: Comes from a wealthy background and assumes the world will cater to her. It's the main reason she struggles as a teacher. Stalker with a Crush: Downplayed; Hector approaches flirting like a competitive sport, hence he's aggressive and unrelenting but not dangerous. My Hovercraft Is Full of Eels: It's an open secret that he cannot speak Chinese at all well, and a couple of these mean a student has to step in to save the course.

characters.name: Series Ten
characters.description: Hot-Blooded: Justin's main character trait. He has no control over his emotions, prone to lashing out violently or making snap decisions that feel right in the moment. Reaches a breaking point whenhe tries to blow up the art room in aMurder-Suicidewhen Tiffany breaks up with him. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing:He's Bonnie's cyberstalker, sending her creepy messages because she never considered him more than a friend. He even tries to get closer to her by pretending to block the stalker with his tech skills. Flirty Stepsiblings: She starts a secret romance with her mother's boyfriend's son, which is part of the reason her mother is so against it. Control Freak: Chaos is not Marco's friend and herding pupils is his least favorite part of the job. Abusive Parents: His and Carrie's mother cared more about her boyfriend than him, disowning Guy for driving him away.

characters.name: Series  11
characters.description: Hidden Depths: A former football referee, which comes out when he offers to officiate a charity match and gets a bit trigger-happy with the red card. Rage Breaking Point: In episode 4 of Series 11, Kelly is in the midst of an emotional breakdown during a communication workshop. When Fred tries to work with her, she jokingly calls him a "tosser with a bald head", leading to him saying to her "I wonder what hole you'll end up in." This comment from Fred is what sends Kelly over the edge, leading to her throwing wooden blocks at him and throwing the thought tree through the window. All Gays Love Theater: Goes to Berlin to study at an art school. Camp Gay: Known for his flamboyance, Kai's family struggles to accept his sexuality.

characters.name: Series  16
characters.description: Unwitting Instigator of Doom: During the careers fair, he wound up mixing up the dairy free cupcakes with the ones that had dairy in them. This soon led to Hope Drake, who is seriously allergic to dairy, consuming one of the dairy-filled cupcakes and dying from a serious reaction. Death of a Child: Tragically dies after suffering from a severe allergic reaction from eating a cupcake with dairy in it.
